LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, MAR-01-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN RESOURCES' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN RESOURCES' DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A PAGA CLAIM FOR ALLEGED VIOLATIONS OF LABOR CODE SECTION 222 AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. JAHAN C. SAGAFI, Esq. and JARED W. GOLDMAN, Esq. of Outten & Golden LLP (Tel: 415-638-8800) for plaintiff Allison Acosta;
Attys. DAVID M. VILLALBA, Esq. and JOANNA Y. THOMAS, Esq. (Tel: 916-324-0512) of/for Defendant California Department of Human Resources;
Attys. STEVEN S. ROSENTHAL, Esq., MARC S. COHEN, Esq., and ALICIA M. CLOUGH, Esq. of Loeb & Loeb LLP (Tel: 310-282-2000) for defendant Betty T. Yee, in her official capacity as State Controller of the State of the California.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BETTY YEE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Betty T. Yee's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot state a PAGA claim for alleged violations of Labor Code section 222 against a public entity.

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN RESOURCES' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant California Department of Human Resources' demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff cannot state a PAGA claim for alleged violations of Labor Code section 222 against a public entity.

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare and submit proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
